title: gomphrena meyeniana var. meyeniana
intro: Gomphrena meyeniana var. meyeniana, also known as Inca wheat or Queñua, is a flowering plant native to South America, specifically in the Andean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Gomphrena meyeniana var. meyeniana include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be dried and consumed as a nutritious and healthy snack.
